statements.impact_statement: SUSE Observability deploys this image as a Kafka broker StatefulSet. The Kafka chart runs /scripts/setup.sh, which execs kafka-server-start.sh with server.properties. The Kafka broker (ZooKeeper mode) does not start an embedded Jetty HTTP server; Jetty is only used by Kafka Connect and Kafka REST workers, which the chart does not start. JMX metrics are scraped by a separate jmx-exporter sidecar image, not by Jetty from this image. The vulnerable HttpParser code path requires an active Jetty HTTP listener processing untrusted requests, which is not present. This statement does not apply to ad hoc use of this image for Kafka Connect, Kafka REST, or a custom command that starts Jetty-backed services.

The vulnerable code is in org.codehaus.plexus.util.Expand.extractFile (Zip Slip during archive extraction). SUSE Observability starts the image as a Kafka broker using kafka-server-start.sh. The broker startup path does not invoke Expand or any plexus-utils archive code; plexus-utils is on the classpath as a transitive dependency of Kafka Connect plugin tooling, which the chart does not start. This statement does not apply to custom commands, manual operator sessions, or Kafka Connect deployments that use this image to unpack untrusted plugin archives.

The vulnerable code is in org.apache.zookeeper.common.ZKTrustManager hostname verification, which falls back to reverse DNS (PTR) when SAN validation fails. The SUSE Observability Kafka chart defaults auth.zookeeper.tls.enabled to false; the broker connects to ZooKeeper over plaintext via KAFKA_ZOOKEEPER_CONNECT and never builds a ZK client SSLContext. ZKTrustManager is therefore not instantiated. This statement does not apply if auth.zookeeper.tls.enabled is enabled or if the image is run with custom ZooKeeper TLS client configuration.

The vulnerable code logs ZooKeeper client configuration values at INFO via ZKConfig. In the SUSE Observability Kafka broker deployment the chart does not inject any sensitive values into the broker's ZooKeeper client configuration: auth.sasl.jaas.zookeeperUser/zookeeperPassword default to empty strings (KAFKA_ZOOKEEPER_USER/PASSWORD are only set when zookeeperUser is non-empty), and auth.zookeeper.tls.enabled is false (so no TLS keystore/truststore paths are mounted or referenced). The logging code path may execute, but the only adversary-relevant input — credentials and key material — is absent, so there is nothing sensitive to leak. This statement does not apply to deployments that enable ZooKeeper SASL or ZooKeeper client TLS for Kafka brokers without re-validating the rendered configuration and log output.

The vulnerable code is in io.netty.channel.epoll (epollOutReady no-op when no pending flush, in the ALLOW_HALF_CLOSURE FIN+RST sequence). SUSE Observability starts this image as a Kafka broker. Kafka broker listeners use Java NIO, not Netty; the ZooKeeper client defaults to ClientCnxnSocketNIO. No part of the supported broker runtime instantiates io.netty.channel.epoll.* channels. NOTE: GHSA-rwm7-x88c-3g2p documents the affected range as netty-transport-native-epoll <= 4.2.12.Final on the 4.2.x branch; the image ships 4.1.125.Final, which is not in the documented affected range, and scanner detection here may itself warrant upstream correction. The not_affected status is asserted on the deployment-context grounds either way. This statement does not apply to custom commands or configuration that explicitly uses Netty epoll from this image.

The vulnerable code is in io.netty.handler.codec.compression.Lz4FrameDecoder.decode (premature buffer allocation based on attacker-controlled header). SUSE Observability starts this image as a Kafka broker. The broker uses Java NIO directly for its listeners, not Netty; Kafka's own LZ4 message compression uses lz4-java rather than Netty's Lz4FrameDecoder. No part of the supported broker runtime instantiates a Netty pipeline containing Lz4FrameDecoder. This statement does not apply to custom commands or configuration that explicitly uses Netty codecs from this image.
